title: Caregiver Sign-In Using Email - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Caregiver Sign-In flow on the AT/AD platform. Covers sign-in form display, email and password validation, successful login with redirect to Caregiver Dashboard/My PwDs, invalid credential handling, account status checks (inactive/suspended/unregistered), forgot password flow, and edge cases including case-insensitive email, account lockout, session timeout, and accessibility.

inScope: Sign-in form display (email, password, sign-in button)
Password masked input and security rules
Successful login authentication and redirect to Caregiver Dashboard / My PwDs
Invalid credentials error handling (generic error message)
Account status checks (inactive, suspended, not registered)
Forgot Password link and password reset flow
Account lockout after multiple failed attempts
Keyboard navigation and screen reader accessibility

testCases.title: Verify sign-in form displays email, password, and sign-in button
testCases.preconditions: User has selected Caregiver account type; Sign-in page is accessible
steps: 2. Verify Email Address field is displayed with label
3. Verify Email Address field is marked as mandatory
4. Verify Password field is displayed with label
5. Verify Password field is marked as mandatory
6. Verify Password field masks input characters
9. Verify sign-up/register link is available for new users
expectedResults: Email Address field is visible with clear label and mandatory indicator
Password field is visible with clear label, mandatory indicator, and masked input
Sign In button is displayed and initially enabled or disabled per design
Forgot Password link is visible and clickable
Sign-up/register option is available for unregistered users
testCases.postconditions: Sign-in form is displayed with all required elements

testCases.title: Verify successful login with valid email and password redirects to Caregiver Dashboard
testCases.preconditions: A registered caregiver account exists with valid credentials
steps: 6. Verify user is redirected to Caregiver Dashboard / My PwDs page
7. Verify the dashboard page loads correctly
8. Verify caregiver name or profile indicator is shown (logged-in state)
9. Verify session is maintained (navigate to another page and back)
expectedResults: System authenticates valid credentials without error
User is redirected to Caregiver Dashboard / My PwDs page
Dashboard loads correctly with caregiver-specific content
Logged-in state is visible (profile name, avatar, or indicator)
Session is maintained across page navigation
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver is logged in with active session on dashboard

testCases.title: Verify invalid credentials show generic error without revealing which field is wrong
steps: 1. Enter valid email but incorrect password
4. Verify error message: 'Invalid email or password. Please try again.'
5. Verify error message does NOT reveal which field is incorrect
7. Enter invalid/unregistered email with any password
9. Verify same generic error message is shown
10. Verify error message is identical for both cases (security best practice)
expectedResults: Login is blocked for incorrect password
Error message: 'Invalid email or password. Please try again.'
Error message does NOT indicate whether email or password is wrong
Same error message shown for unregistered email
Both scenarios produce identical error (no information leakage)
testCases.postconditions: User remains on sign-in page; No session created

testCases.title: Verify password field masks input and enforces security rules
steps: 3. Verify characters are masked (shown as dots or asterisks)
5. Leave password field empty and attempt sign in
6. Verify validation error for empty password
7. Enter password and verify it is accepted
expectedResults: Password characters are masked as they are typed
Show/hide toggle works if present
Empty password triggers validation error
Valid password is accepted for submission
testCases.postconditions: Password field masking and validation verified

testCases.title: Verify sign-in blocked with empty email and password fields
steps: 5. Verify validation errors appear for both fields
6. Fill only email, leave password empty, click Sign In
7. Verify validation error for empty password
8. Clear email, fill only password, click Sign In
9. Verify validation error for empty email
expectedResults: Sign-in is blocked when both fields are empty
Validation errors appear for each empty mandatory field
Sign-in is blocked when only email is provided (no password)
Sign-in is blocked when only password is provided (no email)
Error messages are clear and specific

testCases.title: Verify inactive/suspended account login is blocked with appropriate message
testCases.preconditions: A caregiver account exists with inactive/suspended status
steps: 2. Enter email of inactive/suspended caregiver account
3. Enter correct password for that account
6. Verify appropriate message is displayed indicating account status
7. Verify message does not expose sensitive account details
expectedResults: Login is blocked for inactive account
Appropriate message shown (e.g., 'Your account is inactive. Please contact support.')
Login is blocked for suspended account
Appropriate message shown for suspended status
Messages do not reveal sensitive information
testCases.postconditions: Account status check is verified; No session created for inactive/suspended accounts

testCases.title: Verify unregistered user is prompted to register as Caregiver
testCases.preconditions: Email is not registered in the system
steps: 6. Verify prompt to register as Caregiver is shown
9. Verify user is redirected to caregiver registration page
expectedResults: Login fails for unregistered email
Prompt to register as Caregiver is displayed
Registration link/button is functional
Clicking registration link redirects to caregiver registration page
testCases.postconditions: Unregistered user is guided to registration flow

testCases.title: Verify email login is case-insensitive
testCases.preconditions: A caregiver account is registered with email 'caregiver@example.com'
steps: 11. Verify same account is accessed in all cases
expectedResults: Uppercase email login succeeds (treated as same account)
Mixed case email login succeeds (treated as same account)
Same caregiver dashboard is accessed regardless of email casing
Email comparison is case-insensitive

testCases.title: Verify browser refresh during login does not resubmit credentials
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver sign-in page is displayed with credentials entered
steps: 7. Verify email and password fields are cleared (or retained per browser behavior)
8. Verify no authentication attempt was made on refresh
expectedResults: Browser refresh does not trigger automatic sign-in
Credentials are not resubmitted on page refresh
Sign-in page reloads without errors
No unintended authentication occurs
testCases.postconditions: Browser refresh behavior during login is verified

testCases.title: Verify logged-in session is maintained across page navigation
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with valid credentials
4. Verify user is still logged in (profile indicator visible)
6. Verify user is still logged in
8. Verify session is maintained throughout navigation
9. Open a new tab and navigate to the platform
10. Verify session is shared across tabs (if applicable)
expectedResults: Logged-in state is maintained across all page navigations
Profile indicator remains visible on all pages
No re-authentication required during active session
Session may be shared across browser tabs
testCases.postconditions: Session persistence across navigation is verified

testCases.title: Verify Forgot Password with unregistered email shows appropriate message
steps: 5. Verify no password reset email is sent to unregistered address
6. Verify message does not reveal whether email is registered (security)
expectedResults: System does not reveal whether email is registered or not
Generic message shown (e.g., 'If this email is registered, a reset link has been sent')
No password reset email is sent to unregistered address
No information leakage about account existence
testCases.postconditions: Forgot password security for unregistered emails is verified

AC1_signin_form.description: Sign-in screen displays Email, Password (masked), Sign In button with email format validation

AC2_successful_login.description: Valid credentials authenticate, redirect to Dashboard/My PwDs, session maintained

AC3_invalid_credentials.description: Incorrect credentials blocked with generic error not revealing which field is wrong

AC4_account_status_checks.description: Inactive/suspended blocked with message; unregistered prompted to register

AC5_forgot_password.description: Forgot Password link, reset via email, set new password, retry login

EC_case_insensitive_email.description: Email with different casing treated as same account

EC_browser_refresh.description: Browser refresh during login does not resubmit credentials

assumptions: Caregiver sign-in page is accessible from account type selection (SCRUM-354)
At least one registered caregiver account exists in QA environment
Account lockout feature is configured in QA (if applicable)
Session timeout duration is known for testing
Password reset email delivery is functional in QA
Dark mode toggle is available on the sign-in page
dependencies: SCRUM-354: Account type selection must route to caregiver sign-in
SCRUM-357: Caregiver registration must be functional to create test accounts
Email delivery service must be operational for forgot password tests
Session management infrastructure must be operational

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Does the sign-in page show a 'Register' link for unregistered users, or is the generic error the only response?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_CG_SIGN_008 expected behavior - AC says 'prompt to register' but AC3 says generic error

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_CG_SIGN_001, 002, 003) should be executed first - core sign-in functionality
High priority tests (005, 006, 007, 008, 014) cover validation, account status, and session
Medium priority tests (010, 013, 015) cover edge cases and forgot password

estimated_effort.critical_path: 6-8 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria (1-5) and all edge cases covered
